Title: Browser Threat Alert: High Severity Event
Executive_Summary: A user navigated to file:///Users/akashsingh/Downloads/docs/git/browser-security/tests/mock_remote_browser.html. The system detected an overall severity of High.
Key_Indicators: Large canvas element (>80% viewport) detected with minimal DOM nodes. Possible noVNC/Kasm remote browser stream.
The Visual Analysis indicates the presence of Microsoft Office 365 branding and logos, which suggests an attempt to impersonate a legitimate Microsoft service.
The Final URL is a local file path, which is inconsistent with a legitimate Microsoft Office 365 service, indicating a potential phishing attempt.
The presence of a credential harvesting login form in the visuals further supports the likelihood of a phishing attack.

The use of a local file path for a supposed Microsoft service is a strong indicator of a Browser-in-the-Browser (BitB) attack, where the attacker simulates a legitimate browser window to deceive users.
Recommended_Mitigation: Do not enter any credentials or personal information. Verify the authenticity of the website by checking the URL and ensure it is a legitimate Microsoft domain. Report the incident to your IT security team for further investigation.
